statement.en: Navigating Transitions to Blended Learning: Engagement and Digital Literacy Development amongst First-Year Chinese Undergraduate Students at Transnational English-Medium Universities in China
statement.summary: While studies have examined the effectiveness of blended learning and institutional transitions, little is known about how first-year Chinese undergraduate students navigate the shift from high schools with limited technology-enhanced learning opportunities to blended learning in English-as-a-Medium-of-Instruction (EMI) transnational universities in China, where frequent technology use is both the norm and an expectation. This study addresses this gap by examining students’ transition experience to blended learning with a focus on engagement and digital literacy development. Employing an explanatory sequential mixed-methods design, the study gathered data from 341 survey responses and 20 interviews. Grounded in Astin’s (1993) Input-Environment-Outcome (I-E-O) model and Leontiev’s (1978) se

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is an empirical research study (mixed-methods, n=341 surveys + 20 interviews) examining a contemporary educational transition phenomenon. The work is dated 2026-04-25 (future/preprint status), employs established theoretical frameworks (I-E-O model, activity theory), but findings are not yet validated through peer review or replication. The phenomenon itself (blended learning transitions for Chinese undergraduates at transnational EMI universities) is observable and real, but the specific causal claims and generalizability remain provisional pending publication and external validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 先行する識字と環境の出会いが、言語と技術の落差によって鍛えられる。
